name: Tomatillo-Apple Salsa
recipeIngredient: 24 Fresh tomatillos, hulled and chopped (4 2/3 cups)
4 Canned Tomatillos (18 oz cans), drained and chopped
3 Medium Tart Apples, peeled, cored, and finely chopped (2 cups)
5 Fresh/Canned Jalapeno Peppers, seeded and finely chopped (1/4 to 1/3 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot or kettle, combine the chopped tomatillos, apples, sweet red pepper, cider vinegar, jalapeno peppers, cilantro, and sugar.
2. Add 1 teaspoon of salt.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
4. Reduce the heat and let it simmer, uncovered, for 15 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and ladle the salsa into hot, sterilized half-pint jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace at the top of each jar.
6. Seal the jars with lids.
7. Process the jars in a boiling-water canner for 10 minutes.
8. Let cool to room temperature before storing. Makes 5 half-pints.
